reasoning: The primary statistical anchor from the FRED tool for the short-term forecast gives a 51% combined probability of increase from the baseline 4.11%, driven by recent minor fluctuations despite a modest linear downtrend. We anchor the first resolution date at 0.51. Over the 1 to 6-month horizons, CME FedWatch data and recent news show a high probability of around 2 rate cuts (50 bps total) by December 2025. Given these expectations are priced into forward rates but actual realized spot rates drop as the easing cycle proceeds, the probability of the 6-month yield rising above the current baseline steadily decreases through mid-to-late 2025, settling to ~0.38. Finally, forecasting one year out (March 2026) involves substantial macroeconomic uncertainty regarding whether inflation returns or whether easing shifts. To account for this increased uncertainty at long horizons, the probability moves robustly back toward 0.50.
